title: albizia rosulata subsp. landakensis
intro: Albizia rosulata subsp. landakensis, also known as the Albizia tree, is a deciduous tree species native to the Philippines, characterized by its broad, flat crown and clusters of pink or white flowers. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids and tannins that have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Albizia rosulata subsp. landakensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Traditionally, the bark of the tree is used to make herbal preparations, such as tea and infusions, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
